channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Rivers Court Suspends INC NEC Elections Pending Internal Dispute Resolution
item.description: Rivers State High Court don issue order wey stop Ijaw National Congress (INC) from holding their National Executive Council (NEC) elections wey dem plan for March 7, 2026. Justice S.H. Aprioku grant i...

item.title: Australian authorities provide protection to Iranian women footballers amid safety concerns
item.description: Five members of Iran's women's football team have been granted humanitarian visas in Australia, following fears that remaining team members could be coerced into returning to Iran. The players were un...

item.title: Tanzanian Women Judges Strengthen Justice Reforms to Tackle Gender-Based Violence
item.description: Women judges in Tanzania have joined their peers globally to mark the International Day of Women Judges 2026, reaffirming their dedication to fight gender-based violence (GBV) and promote fair access ...

item.title: Punjab Farmers and Leaders Condemn US-Israel Aggression Against Iran
item.description: On Tuesday, thousands of farmers from Punjab gathered in Barnala district to protest against the recent trade agreement between India and the United States, while also expressing solidarity with Iran ...

item.title: Sir Nick Clegg criticises current university tuition fee system and calls for reforms
item.description: Sir Nick Clegg, former leader of the Liberal Democrats and ex-Deputy Prime Minister, has described the current university tuition fee system in England as 'a mess'. Reflecting on his time in governmen...

item.title: Suliman Carrim Testifies on Business Ties to Matlala and Maumela at Madlanga Commission
item.description: North West businessman Suliman Carrim testified at the Madlanga Commission of Inquiry into his financial dealings with Vusimuzi 'Cat' Matlala and Hangwani Morgan Maumela, both implicated in the Tembis...

item.title: Government to conduct independent audit on austerity measures and implement energy-saving steps
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has directed all federal ministries and divisions to strictly follow the austerity measures announced recently, especially in light of the ongoing global energy crisis tr...

item.title: Obasa joins Commonwealth Parliamentary Association as African Region Executive Member
item.description: The Speaker of Lagos State House of Assembly, Mudashiru Ajayi Obasa, don get appointed as Sub-National Representative for the Commonwealth Parliamentary Association (CPA) African Region Executive Comm...

item.title: Cosmic Voids: Where Dark Energy Dominates and Drives Universe Expansion
item.description: Cosmic voids, the vast empty regions between galaxies and cosmic structures, appear devoid of matter, radiation, dark matter, and other components. However, they are not truly empty. According to quan...

item.title: Mamelodi Sundowns reclaim Betway Premiership lead with 2-0 victory over Orbit College
item.description: Mamelodi Sundowns regained the top position in the Betway Premiership standings after securing a solid 2-0 win against Orbit College FC at Olympia Park Stadium in Rustenburg on Tuesday night, 10 March...

item.title: Farm Fresh defeats Choco Mucho with Tubino scoring against her former team
item.description: In a thrilling match of the Premier Volleyball League (PVL) All-Filipino Conference, Farm Fresh Foxies managed to secure a surprising win against the Choco Mucho Flying Titans. The highlight of the ga...

item.title: Shawn Harris and Clay Fuller advance to runoff in Georgia special election for Greene’s House seat
item.description: Democrat Shawn Harris and Republican Clay Fuller will face each other in a runoff on 7 April to fill the remaining months of Marjorie Taylor Greene’s former U.S. House seat in Georgia after neither se...

item.title: Saskatchewan health-care workers respond to new ‘Patients First’ plan
item.description: Associations representing nurse practitioners and pharmacists in Saskatchewan have expressed optimism regarding the province’s newly-announced 'Patients First' health care plan. Under the plan, nurse ...

item.title: Nairobi City Thunder Signs Nigerian-American Forward Ahead of BAL 2026
item.description: Kenya’s top basketball side, Nairobi City Thunder, have added Nigerian-American power forward Josh Nzeakor to their roster ahead of the Basketball Africa League (BAL) 2026 season. Nzeakor, who comes f...

item.title: Philippines Faces Oil Price Surge Amid Middle East Conflict
item.description: The ongoing conflict between the United States and Iran has caused significant disruptions in global oil supply, directly impacting the Philippines. The crisis intensified after February 28, 2026, whe...

item.title: Porac Mayor Begins 90-Day Suspension Over Unauthorized POGO Permit
item.description: On March 11, 2026, Porac Mayor Jaime Capil in Pampanga began serving a 90-day suspension ordered by a local court due to his approval of a Philippine offshore gaming operator (POGO) hub without proper...

item.title: Nigeria Police Assigns Portfolios to Newly Appointed DIGs
item.description: On Monday, the eight newly promoted Deputy Inspectors General of Police (DIGs) for Nigeria don officially collect their portfolios, as The Nation reports. Inspector-General of Police, IGP Olatunji Dis...

item.title: Women Judges in Tanzania Strengthen Efforts to Protect GBV Survivors
item.description: In Dodoma, women judges across Tanzania have once again affirmed their strong commitment to fight gender-based violence (GBV) and protect victims more effectively. The reaffirmation came during the 20...

item.title: AI Tools Challenge Traditional Indian IT Outsourcing Model
item.description: On 4th February, the $300 billion Indian IT sector experienced a notable shake-up as the stock index for IT firms fell almost 6% after Anthropic launched its Claude Cowork agentic plugin. This AI plug...

item.title: Kunle Afolayan Flags Diesel Costs Affecting KAP Film Village Operations
item.description: Nollywood filmmaker Kunle Afolayan don express serious wahala over the high cost of diesel wey dey affect him business operations. According to him, him KAP Film Village and Resort dey spend around ₦1...
